accounts.note: linked account; reconciles to the bank feed
Razorpay owes us; should clear ~0 at period end
contra-revenue; goodwill credits / price adjustments (credit-note treatment) reduce revenue here
loss recognised on a lost dispute
cash-on-delivery: the courier collected our cash at the door and owes it to us; clears ~0 once remitted
the courier's per-shipment COD collection fee
return-to-origin (RTO) shipping the courier charges back on undelivered parcels

entry_types.name: dtc_sale
entry_types.doc: Customer purchase captured by Razorpay: gross receivable from Razorpay, split into net product revenue and output GST.

entry_types.name: razorpay_settlement
entry_types.doc: Razorpay deposits net cash to the bank, having netted its fee and GST-on-fee out of the gross receivable. Posted via reconcileTx (bank is the linked account).

entry_types.name: refund_reversal
entry_types.doc: Customer refund: reverse net revenue (into the contra-revenue account) and the output GST, clearing the receivable for the refunded amount.

entry_types.name: price_adjustment
entry_types.doc: Goodwill credit / price adjustment refund (credit note): reduce revenue via discounts-allowances and the output GST, clearing the receivable for the credited amount. Truth-only in v1 agent policy: the agent escalates goodwill rather than booking it.

entry_types.name: chargeback_loss
entry_types.doc: Lost dispute: recognise the loss and reverse the output GST, with cash leaving the bank.

entry_types.name: cod_sale
entry_types.doc: Cash-on-delivery sale, recognised at DELIVERY (revenue-at-delivery policy): the courier has collected our cash at the door, so the gross is receivable from the courier, split into net product revenue and output GST. Mirrors dtc_sale on the COD rail.

entry_types.name: cod_remittance
entry_types.doc: Courier wires the collected COD cash to the bank, having netted its per-shipment collection fee and GST-on-fee out of the gross it collected. Mirrors razorpay_settlement on the COD rail: net_deposit + fee + gst_on_fee == the gross it clears off the COD receivable.

entry_types.name: rto_fee
entry_types.doc: Return-to-origin fee the courier charges on an undelivered parcel, netted out of a remittance. Recognises the reverse-logistics cost and its recoverable input GST, clearing the COD receivable for the deducted amount. The investigate agent books this only when the deduction matches the courier's rate card.

entry_types.name: weight_adjustment
entry_types.doc: Courier weight-dispute deduction netted out of a remittance (a reweigh charge). Clears the COD receivable for the deducted amount against reverse-logistics. Truth books it; the agent escalates an unverified weight deduction rather than booking it, since it has no rate-card basis.
